bf165ba: runtime questions and non-imperative best/default language remain unresolved; deterministic selection requires an explicit imperative delegation
b4c7c6a: runtime children receive an allowlisted environment plus only the selected credential names
b4c7c6a: configured credential values are redacted from subprocess stdout, stderr, and errors before display
b4c7c6a: Codex and Claude verification consumes structured assistant output and requires exact WORKFRAME_OK
b4c7c6a: OpenAI and OpenRouter verification parses provider JSON and requires an exact assistant response
384876c: adversarial regressions cover secret-bearing stderr, argv echo, refusal or diagnostic text, and runtime-selection questions
implementer evidence: reconstructed package reported 34 passing tests on Linux x64 / Node v22.16.0
implementer evidence: packed workframe-0.2.1.tgz clean-install smoke checks passed
implementer evidence: real PTY Ctrl+C and Ctrl+D at consent exited safely
reviewer source inspection and targeted exact-parser reproduction against head 2b85c70d3fb9302dc435c45459ac698ca46a05f6
compare main...automation/wf-cli-001-socratic-seed-v3: branch diverged, 18 commits ahead and 9 behind; PR mergeable=false
exact-head status: only Vercel success was reported; no pull-request workflow run was available for 2b85c70d3fb9302dc435c45459ac698ca46a05f6
80c98cb: canonical backlog reconciled narrowly to todo; non-CLI rows unchanged
no live provider call, package publication, installation mutation, or non-CLI code change
acceptance: select one existing inference path conversationally without guessing ambiguity
require separate billing-disclosed consent before a minimal verification call
after a verified path, collect preferred name and first objective
print a bounded memory-only mirror without filesystem or installation mutation
package-local and packed-artifact checks pass without live credentials

failures: questioning or qualified language could authorize a provider call
authority-boundary tests did not exercise the complete flow
EOF and interruption were not handled deterministically

verified_repairs: consent questions and qualifiers failed closed
help text stated the direct credential transmission boundary truthfully
failures: Codex and Claude failure stderr was printed without secret redaction while child processes inherited the full environment
Codex and Claude verification accepted successful output that merely echoed WORKFRAME_OK from the prompt
OpenAI and OpenRouter verification substring-matched the raw HTTP response body
candidate selection treated best/default questions as delegation
repository CI remained red at aggregate Harness verify

review_focus: independently reconstruct the current package and rerun all 34 tests
prove configured credential values cannot escape through subprocess or provider diagnostics
prove argv echo and non-assistant output cannot verify Codex or Claude
prove refusal or diagnostic text cannot verify OpenAI or OpenRouter
prove best/default questions remain unresolved without explicit imperative delegation
rerun packed-artifact, PTY, and available CI checks from the exact current head

verified_repairs: structured exact-response parsing replaced prompt-token substring matching
known secret-like environment values are redacted from displayed subprocess diagnostics
consent questions and qualifiers remain fail closed
consent-prompt EOF and Ctrl+C settlement paths are present
failures: Ctrl+C after consent does not abort a pending provider verification: runInteractiveFlow awaits verify without an abort signal, HTTP requests use only timeout signals, and CLI verification uses synchronous spawnSync with up to a 90-second timeout
hedged path answers such as 'Maybe Claude.', 'Probably Codex.', and 'I guess OpenRouter.' are treated as selected rather than ambiguous
Codex and Claude candidates opportunistically receive configured API keys while billing text says account or provider, so the exact credential and payer are not deterministically disclosed
the inference-child allowlist includes DOCKER_HOST, DOCKER_CONTEXT, DOCKER_CONFIG, and SSH_AUTH_SOCK, exposing unrelated high-authority handles to the model runtime
the pull-request branch is nine commits behind current main, is diverged, and is not mergeable
no package or repository CI run is attached to the exact reviewed head
required_remediation: thread one cancellation signal from the terminal through runInteractiveFlow into verification; abort fetch requests and replace synchronous runtime execution with a cancellable child process that terminates on Ctrl+C
add verification-phase interruption tests proving the provider call or child process is cancelled promptly and no Socratic prompt follows
treat hedging and uncertainty markers as unresolved path selection, with regressions for maybe, perhaps, probably, and I guess
represent account-backed and API-key-backed runtime paths as distinct candidates and disclose the exact billing source before consent; never inject a provider key opportunistically
split discovery and inference environment allowlists and remove Docker and SSH authority handles from inference children
rebuild the implementation branch from current main and rerun package, packed-artifact, PTY, Windows-semantics, and available CI checks from the exact repaired head
current_review.canonical_backlog_reconciliation: resolved at 80c98cbad32ff9f0e68f0eb9990add2d6ff07a85: exactly one campaign remains, WF-CLI-001 is todo, WF-CLI-002 is dependency-gated, and the commit diff changes only WF-CLI rows

items.title: Truthful runtime and provider capability graph
Explicit apply and rollback authority gates

invariants: Do not modify packages/create-workframe, apps, services, infra, or existing runtime behavior.
Do not read, print, persist, or transmit credential values except directly to the explicitly selected provider after consent.
Do not overwrite, adopt, or mutate an existing setup without explicit approval of a concrete immutable plan.
Do not use a Workframe-hosted or implicit cloud fallback.
Publication remains a separate reviewed release decision.
